title: Conflict Stages, Semantic Resolution, and Rerere · Guided Repository Studio
summary: A pairing session predicts each repository-state transition before building the first three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier for conflict stages, semantic resolution, and rerere. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.
Distinguish content, add/add, delete/modify, rename, directory/file, binary, mode, submodule, and recursive conflicts and select evidence for each.
Reconstruct intent from base and both histories, produce a semantic combined result, and test invariants that neither side tested alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Case G14 is a workshop in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-conflict-regression-handoff-vrefrj" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Conflict Regression Handoff into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-conflict-regression-handoff-vrefrj
# scenario-key: trace-vrefrj probe-jrferv boundary-efrjvr owner-vrefrjx fault-vrefrjz
# before-ref: main=a11cevr candidate=b00b1rj
# hypothesis: the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict
git ls-files --unmerged
# expected: stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction
# observation: all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context
# changed-case: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail
# after-ref: main=a11cevr candidate=c0ffeej
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer
# transfer-limit: rerere output remains a proposal and must pass semantic changed-case verification
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record conflict cause, chosen invariant, rerere provenance, changed-case tests, and abort or continuation evidence for future integrators.

hints: Name the Gita Conflict Regression Handoff precondition or invariant that the workshop case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G23 is a workshop in a disposable repository. the clean-looking file hides a stage-level rename and content conflict. The learner must predict stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, and stage 3 theirs identify exact objects for reconstruction, run git ls-files --unmerged, explain all three policy blobs differ and the base path proves the rename context, test this changed condition: reuse a recorded resolution on a changed conflict and require the combined behavior test to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: record MERGE_HEAD and both branch tips before aborting or continuing the sequencer. Competency probe: Record conflict cause, chosen invariant, rerere provenance, changed-case tests, and abort or continuation evidence for future integrators.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Once rerere applies cleanly, human and behavioral review are redundant.
